articles.title: FBI Investigates After 10 Scientists Linked to US Nuclear Secrets Go Missing or Die
articles.summary: The FBI has opened a probe into the mysterious disappearances and deaths of at least 10 scientists connected to US nuclear secrets and rocket technology. The investigation was sparked by tabloid reports from The Daily Mail linking the cases together. The pattern has raised serious alarm inside US intelligence and law enforcement circles.
articles.content:
At least 10 scientists with connections to US nuclear secrets and rocket technology have gone missing or died under unexplained circumstances over the past few years. Now the FBI is formally investigating whether these cases are connected, according to Ars Technica. That is not a small thing. A federal probe into a possible conspiracy targeting people who hold some of America's most sensitive technical knowledge is about as serious as it gets.

What makes the story stranger is how it came to a head. The investigation was partly spurred by reporting from The Daily Mail, a British tabloid not typically associated with kicking off FBI probes. But the pattern the tabloid pieced together was apparently compelling enough that US authorities took notice and opened their own inquiry. The cases individually might have looked like accidents, suicides, or unrelated disappearances. Lined up together, the picture looked different enough to warrant federal attention.

The scientists involved are described as having ties to nuclear secrets and rocket technology, which puts them squarely in the category of people that foreign intelligence services would love to silence or recruit. Counterintelligence experts have long warned that scientists and engineers with classified knowledge are high-value targets, particularly for state-sponsored actors. The FBI has not publicly named suspects or a country of interest, but the nature of the victims makes the usual suspects obvious.

This story is still developing and a lot of key details remain unknown, including how each person died or disappeared and what specific connections between the cases the FBI has identified. What is clear is that 10 people with access to sensitive US technology are gone under circumstances shadowy enough to trigger a federal conspiracy investigation. That is the kind of story that deserves close attention as more information comes out.
keyPoints: At least 10 scientists linked to US nuclear and rocket secrets have gone missing or died in recent years
The FBI has opened a formal probe into whether the cases are connected
The investigation was partly triggered by reporting from The Daily Mail
Scientists with classified knowledge are known high-value targets for foreign intelligence services
The FBI has not yet named suspects or a country of interest publicly

articles.title: Timothée Chalamet Invests in Urban Jürgensen in His First Ever Company Partnership
articles.summary: Timothée Chalamet has taken a minority stake in Urban Jürgensen, a historic Danish watchmaker founded in 1773, and will serve as the brand's creative advisor. This is the first time Chalamet has formally partnered with and invested in a company rather than simply fronting a campaign. The move signals a serious step into the luxury world for one of Hollywood's most culturally influential young stars.
articles.content:
Timothée Chalamet is not just doing ads anymore. The actor has taken an actual ownership stake in Urban Jürgensen, a family-owned Danish watchmaker with roots going back to 1773, and will serve as its creative advisor. Business of Fashion confirmed this is his first formal partnership role in any company.

Urban Jürgensen is not a household name in the way that Rolex or Patek Philippe is, but in serious watch circles it carries enormous weight. The brand was founded in Copenhagen and has spent over 250 years making high-end mechanical timepieces for collectors who care more about heritage and craft than marketing flash. That quietness is actually part of the appeal, and it fits Chalamet's image pretty well.

Chalamet has been circling the luxury space for a while. He has been a face of Blancpain watches and has long been photographed in archival and high fashion. But being a face is different from being an investor and creative voice inside a company. As creative advisor, he will have real input into how Urban Jürgensen presents itself to the world, not just show up for a photoshoot.

For Urban Jürgensen, the upside is obvious. Chalamet is arguably the most culturally magnetic young actor working right now. His fanbase skews young, and that is exactly the audience that legacy watch brands are desperate to reach without losing their existing collectors. The challenge is doing that without the brand feeling like it sold out.

This is also part of a broader trend of actors and musicians moving from endorsement deals into actual equity stakes. It gives them more creative control, more long-term financial upside, and frankly more interesting stories to tell. Chalamet investing in a 250-year-old Danish watchmaker is a much more compelling narrative than Chalamet wearing a watch in a magazine. Urban Jürgensen just became a lot easier to Google.
keyPoints: Timothée Chalamet has taken a minority investment in Urban Jürgensen, a Danish watchmaker founded in 1773
This is his first ever formal partnership role in a company, not just a brand deal
He will serve as creative advisor, giving him real input into the brand's direction
Urban Jürgensen is a respected name in high-end watch collecting with over 250 years of history
The move is part of a wider trend of celebrities taking equity stakes rather than just endorsement fees

articles.title: Michael B. Jordan and Austin Butler Star in IMAX-Shot Miami Vice '85
articles.summary: Universal Pictures has confirmed Michael B. Jordan and Austin Butler as the leads of Miami Vice '85, a period reboot of the iconic 1980s crime franchise. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, the film will be shot in IMAX and set in 1985 Miami. Jordan plays Tubbs and Butler plays Crockett, the legendary detective duo at the heart of the original series.
articles.content:
Universal Pictures is officially moving forward with Miami Vice '85, and the casting alone is enough to get excited about. Michael B. Jordan takes on the role of Ricardo Tubbs and Austin Butler steps into the shoes of Sonny Crockett, the two detectives who made the original Miami Vice a cultural phenomenon in the 1980s.

For anyone who missed the original, Miami Vice was a groundbreaking NBC crime drama that ran from 1984 to 1990. It basically invented the cool, sun-soaked aesthetic that defined an entire decade of television. Don Johnson and Philip Michael Thomas played Crockett and Tubbs respectively, and the show was as famous for its pastel suits and synth soundtrack as it was for its crime stories. Michael Mann later directed a film adaptation in 2006 starring Colin Farrell and Jamie Foxx.

This time around, Joseph Kosinski is in the director's chair. Kosinski is the man behind Top Gun: Maverick, which became one of the biggest blockbusters of 2022, so he knows how to handle slick action with real cinematic weight. The decision to shoot Miami Vice '85 in IMAX is a big deal. It means the film is being built for the biggest possible screen experience, which suggests Universal is betting on this one hard.

Setting the story in 1985 is a smart move. It locks the film into the original era without trying to update or modernise the premise, which means the fashion, the music, and the vibe can all be authentically 80s. With Jordan and Butler both at the peak of their cultural relevance right now, Miami Vice '85 already feels like one of the most anticipated films on the horizon.
keyPoints: Michael B. Jordan plays Tubbs and Austin Butler plays Crockett in Miami Vice '85
Joseph Kosinski, director of Top Gun: Maverick, is helming the film
The film will be shot in IMAX and set in 1985
Universal Pictures is officially moving forward with the project
The original Miami Vice ran on NBC from 1984 to 1990

articles.title: Microsoft Launches Vibe Working in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint This Week
articles.summary: Microsoft is rolling out Agent Mode inside Word, Excel, and PowerPoint starting this week, bringing what the company has been calling vibe working to millions of Office users. Agent Mode is a significantly more powerful version of the existing Microsoft Copilot experience, letting the AI handle multi-step tasks inside documents and spreadsheets with far less hand-holding. This is one of the biggest upgrades to the Microsoft Office suite in years.
articles.content:
Microsoft has officially started rolling out Agent Mode across Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and the company has been describing the experience with a phrase that is going to stick: vibe working. The idea is that instead of typing out precise instructions, you describe roughly what you want and Agent Mode figures out the steps to get there. It is a meaningful step up from what Microsoft Copilot already does inside Office apps.

Here is the key difference between Agent Mode and the existing Microsoft Copilot features. Standard Copilot responds to individual prompts, like a very fast assistant you have to direct at every turn. Agent Mode can take a broader goal and handle the sequence of tasks needed to complete it, moving through a document or spreadsheet more like an employee who understood the assignment than a tool waiting for its next command. That shift in dynamic is what Microsoft is branding as vibe working.

For everyday Microsoft Office users this could genuinely change how work feels. Imagine telling Excel to pull together a financial summary from three different worksheets, format it, flag the anomalies, and draft a plain-English explanation for a non-technical audience. Agent Mode is built to handle that entire chain rather than making you manage each step yourself. Microsoft has been building toward this for a while, with Copilot arriving in Office apps in 2023 as the foundation that Agent Mode is now sitting on top of.

The rollout is happening this week, which means most users with Microsoft 365 subscriptions should see it arrive in their apps soon. The timing is interesting given the broader race in AI-powered productivity tools. Google has been pushing similar features through Gemini inside Google Workspace, and the competition between these two giants over who owns the future of how office work gets done is very much live. Microsoft calling it vibe working is a deliberate attempt to plant a phrase in the cultural conversation, the way terms like cloud computing or the paperless office once did.

Whether vibe working actually becomes the shorthand people use remains to be seen. But the underlying technology is real, the rollout is happening now, and if Agent Mode delivers even half of what Microsoft is promising, it is going to change how a lot of people spend their Thursdays.
keyPoints: Microsoft is rolling out Agent Mode in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint this week
Agent Mode is what Microsoft has been calling vibe working, a more powerful version of Copilot
Unlike standard Copilot, Agent Mode can handle multi-step tasks from a single broad instruction
Microsoft 365 subscribers should see the update arrive in their apps imminently
The move is a direct response to Google pushing similar AI features through Gemini in Google Workspace

articles.title: Judge Dismisses Sean Diddy Combs's $100 Million Defamation Suit Against NBC
articles.summary: A judge has dismissed Sean Diddy Combs's $100 million defamation lawsuit against NBCUniversal, the suit he filed after Peacock aired the documentary Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy last year. Combs, who is currently imprisoned while awaiting trial on federal sex trafficking and racketeering charges, had argued the documentary painted a false and damaging picture of him. The dismissal means NBCUniversal and Peacock face no liability over the film.
articles.content:
Sean Diddy Combs filed a $100 million defamation lawsuit against NBCUniversal after Peacock aired Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy in 2025. The documentary examined allegations against Combs and was one of several high-profile media projects that emerged after his September 2024 arrest on federal charges including sex trafficking and racketeering. Combs has pleaded not guilty to those charges and remains imprisoned while his criminal case moves forward.

A judge has now dismissed the defamation suit entirely. The ruling means Combs's legal team failed to convince the court that Peacock's documentary crossed the line from commentary and journalism into actionable defamation. Defamation cases require proving that false statements of fact were made with a degree of fault, and courts historically give significant protection to documentary filmmakers covering public figures, especially ones already at the centre of major criminal proceedings.

For NBCUniversal and Peacock, this is a clean win. The $100 million figure was always eye-catching, and the lawsuit had the potential to create a chilling effect on documentary journalism about powerful figures facing criminal charges. The dismissal signals that courts are willing to protect that kind of reporting.

For Combs, this is another setback outside his criminal case. His legal team has been active on multiple fronts since his arrest, but the defamation route against Peacock has now been shut down completely. His criminal trial remains the central battleground, and the outcome there will define how this chapter of music industry history is remembered.
keyPoints: A judge dismissed Sean Diddy Combs's $100 million defamation lawsuit against NBCUniversal
The suit was filed after Peacock aired the documentary Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy
Combs is currently imprisoned awaiting trial on federal sex trafficking and racketeering charges
The ruling is a significant win for Peacock and documentary journalism more broadly
Combs has pleaded not guilty to all criminal charges

articles.title: Ferrari's First EV, the Luce, Debuts in Rome With an $823,000 Price Tag
articles.summary: Ferrari is preparing to unveil its first ever electric vehicle, reportedly named the Luce, at a debut event in Rome. The four-seater EV is expected to start at around $823,000 USD, making it one of the most expensive electric cars ever produced. This is a landmark moment for Ferrari, a brand that has long resisted going fully electric.
articles.content:
Ferrari is finally going electric, and naturally, it is doing so in the most Ferrari way possible. The car is reportedly called the Luce, it seats four people, and it is expected to carry a starting price of around $823,000 USD. That is not a typo.

The Luce is set to make its public debut in Rome, which is a fitting stage for what Ferrari is clearly positioning as a genuinely historic moment for the brand. Ferrari has spent years being one of the loudest voices of scepticism about electric vehicles in the high-performance car world, so the fact that the Luce exists at all is a big deal. This is the company crossing a line it once seemed determined never to cross.

The $823,000 price point puts the Luce in rarefied air even by Ferrari standards. For context, Ferrari's current lineup starts around $200,000 for a base model and climbs from there. At $823,000, the Luce is being positioned alongside ultra-exclusive hypercars rather than Ferrari's more accessible sports cars. That framing makes sense if Ferrari wants to make sure its first EV is seen as a pinnacle product rather than a concession to market trends.

The four-seat layout is also worth noting. Ferrari rarely builds four-seaters. The Purosangue SUV, launched in 2022, was the brand's first proper four-seat car in decades and caused plenty of debate among purists. The Luce being a four-seater suggests Ferrari sees its EV as a grand tourer aimed at a slightly different buyer than its traditional two-seat sports cars. All eyes are now on Rome for the full reveal.
keyPoints: Ferrari's first electric vehicle is reportedly named the Luce
The Luce is a four-seater with an expected starting price of $823,000 USD
Ferrari plans to debut the Luce at an event in Rome
This marks Ferrari's first fully electric production car
The price places the Luce among the most expensive EVs ever made

articles.title: McLaren Sign 11-Year-Old Harry Williams, Two Years Younger Than Lewis Hamilton Was
articles.summary: McLaren have signed 11-year-old Harry Williams to their young driver programme, making him the youngest signing in the team's history. To put that in perspective, Lewis Hamilton was 13 when he joined McLaren's programme, and he went on to become a seven-time Formula 1 world champion. Harry Williams is now two years younger than that benchmark.
articles.content:
McLaren have just made the most eye-catching signing in junior motorsport in a long time. Harry Williams, aged just 11, has joined McLaren's young driver programme, making him the youngest person the team has ever signed.

The comparison that everyone is reaching for is Lewis Hamilton. Hamilton, now widely considered the greatest Formula 1 driver of all time with seven world championships to his name, was 13 years old when McLaren signed him to their junior programme back in 1998. That was already considered young. Harry Williams is doing it two years earlier.

McLaren's young driver programme is the pipeline that feeds talent toward Formula 1. It gives young drivers access to coaching, resources, and the kind of structured development that can shape a career over many years. For a team that produced Hamilton, signing Williams at 11 is a statement. McLaren clearly see something exceptional in him and want to get ahead of any competition for his development.

For context, Formula 1 drivers cannot compete in F1 until they are 18, so Harry Williams has a long road ahead. But the karting and junior single-seater categories are where careers are made or broken, and having McLaren in your corner at age 11 is an enormous advantage. The team will be guiding his path through those formative years.

It is worth stepping back and appreciating how unusual this moment is. Elite sports academies sign young talent all the time, but breaking a Lewis Hamilton record is a different kind of headline. Whether Williams fulfils the promise McLaren clearly see in him remains to be seen, but right now he is the most talked about 11-year-old in motorsport.
keyPoints: McLaren have signed 11-year-old Harry Williams, their youngest ever signing
Lewis Hamilton was 13 when McLaren signed him, making Williams two years younger at signing
Hamilton went on to win seven Formula 1 world championships after joining McLaren's programme
F1 drivers must be at least 18 to compete, so Williams has years of junior development ahead
McLaren's young driver programme is designed to develop talent from grassroots karting to Formula 1

articles.title: Coyote vs. Acme Is Finally Coming Out After Warner Bros. Shelved It in 2023
articles.summary: Coyote vs. Acme, the live-action and animated film that Warner Bros. controversially shelved in 2023, is finally getting a release through new distributor Ketchup Entertainment. Ketchup Entertainment has already released a trailer, giving audiences their first real look at a film that caused a massive outcry when it was buried before anyone could see it. The original decision by Warner Bros. to write off the completed film for a tax benefit rather than release it sparked widespread anger across Hollywood and among fans.
articles.content:
One of Hollywood's strangest recent stories is getting a happy ending. Coyote vs. Acme, a completed live-action and animated film based on the Looney Tunes character Wile E. Coyote, was shelved by Warner Bros. in 2023 before it ever reached cinemas. Now, thanks to Ketchup Entertainment stepping in as distributor, the film is actually going to be seen by the public.

To understand why this was such a big deal, you need to know what Warner Bros. did. Rather than releasing the finished film, Warner Bros. decided to write it off as a tax deduction. That means the studio essentially chose to make money by not releasing it rather than taking a chance on it at the box office. The move caused an enormous backlash. Filmmakers, actors, and industry workers were furious because it felt like creative work was being treated as a financial asset to be discarded rather than a piece of art to be shared.

Ketchup Entertainment, which specialises in picking up and distributing films that bigger studios have passed on, stepped in to rescue Coyote vs. Acme from the vault. The fact that Ketchup Entertainment has now released a trailer is significant. It means the film has cleared whatever legal and financial hurdles came with changing distributors, and an actual release is now on the way.

The trailer is reportedly strong, which makes the original Warner Bros. decision look even stranger in hindsight. Coyote vs. Acme is built around the premise of Wile E. Coyote suing the Acme Corporation for all the defective products that failed to help him catch Road Runner, which is exactly the kind of clever, self-aware concept that audiences tend to love. If the trailer is any indication, Warner Bros. may have walked away from something genuinely good. The film's eventual release will be one of the more unusual comeback stories in recent Hollywood memory.
keyPoints: Coyote vs. Acme was shelved by Warner Bros. in 2023 as a tax write-off rather than released
Ketchup Entertainment has acquired the film and will distribute it
A trailer has been released, giving audiences their first look at the finished film
The original shelving decision caused widespread outrage across the film industry
The film follows Wile E. Coyote suing Acme Corporation for faulty products

articles.title: Anthropic Promised Fully Autonomous AI Employees by Today. Where Are They?
articles.summary: Anthropic predicted that fully autonomous AI agents would be transforming the business world by 22 April 2026. That day has arrived, and the reality looks a lot messier than the hype. Here is an honest look at how far AI agents have actually come.
articles.content:
Back when Anthropic made its bold prediction, the vision was vivid. AI agents that could take on full employee roles, independently handle complex tasks, make decisions, and churn through workloads without a human holding their hand at every step. The kind of thing that would fundamentally change how companies hire, operate, and compete. The deadline was today, 22 April 2026.

So here we are. And while AI agents have made real progress, the picture is more complicated than Anthropic's confident forecast suggested. Tools from Anthropic and its competitors have gotten genuinely impressive at handling chunks of work, browsing the web, writing code, and managing multi-step tasks. But the fully autonomous, fire-and-forget AI employee that was promised? That version still has serious gaps. Agents still hallucinate, still get confused by edge cases, and still need meaningful human oversight to avoid costly mistakes.

The interesting thing is that Anthropic is not alone in having oversold the timeline. The whole AI industry has been making bold claims about agentic AI for the past two years, and the gap between the demo and the deployment has been a recurring theme. Companies that have actually tried to hand real workflows to AI agents report that the last 20 percent of reliability is brutally hard to close.

None of this means the prediction was entirely wrong, just early. Agentic AI is clearly the direction everything is moving, and Anthropic's Claude models are genuinely central to that push. But today is a useful gut check. Hype has a way of setting clocks forward, and the business world is still waiting for the fully autonomous AI colleague to actually show up.
keyPoints: Anthropic predicted fully autonomous AI employees would be widespread by 22 April 2026
AI agents have improved but still require significant human oversight to work reliably
Hallucinations and edge case failures remain major barriers to true autonomy
The whole AI industry has consistently oversold agentic AI timelines
The direction is right but the deadline was premature

articles.title: N3on Paid $1.4 Million to Clippers in Five Weeks to Go Viral
articles.summary: Kick streamer N3on has revealed he spent $1.4 million paying a team of video clippers over just five weeks to flood social media with his content and manufacture viral moments. The admission pulls back the curtain on how some creators engineer their own popularity rather than letting it happen organically. It is one of the biggest public confessions of paid virality the streaming world has ever seen.
articles.content:
N3on, the Kick streamer known for his chaotic and controversial content, has admitted to spending $1.4 million on an organised army of video clippers in just five weeks. Clippers are people who take highlights from long streams and edit them into short, shareable clips designed to spread across TikTok, Twitter, and YouTube. It is a common behind-the-scenes tactic in the streaming world, but nobody has ever publicly admitted to paying this much for it.

The scale here is genuinely staggering. $1.4 million over five weeks works out to roughly $280,000 a week being poured into content distribution. N3on is essentially treating virality like a product launch, hiring a workforce dedicated entirely to making sure his face appears everywhere online whether people seek him out or not.

This confession matters because it exposes the machinery behind what looks like organic internet fame. When you see a clip of N3on blowing up on your timeline, there is a good chance someone was paid specifically to put it there. For fans who thought they were stumbling across something genuinely popular, that is a significant reframe.

For the broader creator economy, this is a warning shot. If spending millions on clippers becomes the standard playbook for growth on Kick and beyond, smaller creators without that kind of budget are competing on a fundamentally uneven field. N3on may have been candid about it, but plenty of others are almost certainly doing the same thing and staying quiet.
keyPoints: N3on spent $1.4 million paying video clippers over just five weeks
Clippers edit stream highlights into short viral clips for platforms like TikTok and YouTube
The spend works out to around $280,000 per week on manufactured virality
N3on made the admission publicly, which is rare in the creator world
The tactic raises questions about fairness for smaller creators without big budgets

articles.title: Japan Is Paying People to Download Dating Apps to Fix Its Birth Rate
articles.summary: Kochi prefecture in Japan is offering subsidies to residents who use dating apps, in a direct attempt to reverse the country's falling birth rate. The initiative aims to remove the cost barrier that stops people from signing up and finding partners. Japan has been battling one of the lowest birth rates in the world for years, and this is one of its most hands-on fixes yet.
articles.content:
Japan has tried a lot of things to get people dating. Matchmaking events, government-run meet-ups, cash bonuses for having babies. But Kochi prefecture just took it a step further by offering to subsidise the actual cost of dating apps for its residents.

The idea is straightforward. Dating apps work. But a lot of people, especially younger ones with tighter budgets, do not want to pay for subscriptions. Kochi is stepping in to cover some of that cost, hoping that if the financial barrier disappears, more people will sign up, meet someone, and eventually start families.

Japan's birth rate has been in serious trouble for decades. The country recorded fewer than 800,000 births in recent years, a historic low, and the government has declared it a near-crisis situation. Kochi is one of Japan's smaller, more rural prefectures, which makes the problem even more acute there. Young people move to cities, populations shrink, and the cycle gets worse.

What makes this move interesting is that it treats dating apps not as a frivolous luxury but as actual public infrastructure. That is a real shift in how governments are thinking about loneliness and population decline. Whether subsidised swiping actually translates into more babies is a completely different question. But the fact that a government is willing to fund it at all says a lot about how seriously Japan is taking this problem.
keyPoints: Kochi prefecture in Japan is subsidising dating app costs for residents
The goal is to reduce financial barriers to finding a partner and boost the birth rate
Japan recorded fewer than 800,000 births in recent years, a historic low
This is one of the most direct government interventions into dating culture seen anywhere
Whether it actually increases births remains to be seen
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Japan birth rate decline government policy

articles.title: Manchester City Go Top as Burnley Are Relegated Back to the Championship
articles.summary: Manchester City beat Burnley at Turf Moor on Wednesday to move to the top of the Premier League table, while the result confirmed Burnley's relegation back to the Championship. Pep Guardiola's Manchester City are now in pole position in the title race with just a handful of games left. For Burnley, it is a swift and painful return to the second tier after coming back up.
articles.content:
Manchester City picked the perfect time for a big win. A victory at Turf Moor on Wednesday night sent Pep Guardiola's side to the top of the Premier League table, with the season entering its final stretch. The timing could not be better for a club that has been relentlessly chasing down the teams above them.

For Burnley, the same result meant the end of the road in the top flight. Their relegation back to the Championship was confirmed by the defeat, ending what turned out to be a short stay back in the Premier League. Burnley had earned promotion to get here, but the gap in quality proved too much to close over the course of the season.

Manchester City's charge up the table has been one of the stories of the second half of this Premier League season. Guardiola, who has managed Manchester City since 2016 and won multiple Premier League titles with the club, has seen his side grind their way back into contention after a difficult earlier stretch this campaign. Getting to the top of the table with games still to play is exactly where they want to be.

The Premier League title race is now wide open heading into the final weeks. Manchester City sitting top does not guarantee anything, but momentum and pressure on rivals are real factors at this stage of the season. For neutrals, it is shaping up to be a nervy and entertaining finish.
keyPoints: Manchester City beat Burnley at Turf Moor to go top of the Premier League
Burnley's relegation back to the Championship was confirmed by the result
Pep Guardiola's Manchester City are now in prime position with the season nearing its end
The title race is wide open heading into the final weeks of the Premier League season

articles.title: Jurassic Park Now Has an Official Fragrance That Is 65 Million Years in the Making
articles.summary: A Jurassic Park branded fragrance has officially launched, marketed with the tagline that it is 65 million years in the making, a nod to the age of the dinosaurs at the heart of Steven Spielberg's 1993 classic film. The scent is available to buy now and is aimed squarely at fans who want to literally smell like one of cinema's most beloved blockbusters. It is the latest example of legacy Hollywood franchises finding surprising new life in the beauty and lifestyle space.
articles.content:
Jurassic Park, Steven Spielberg's 1993 dinosaur blockbuster that launched one of the biggest film franchises in Hollywood history, now has its own official fragrance. The perfume leans hard into the film's legacy with the tagline that it is 65 million years in the making, a playful reference to the prehistoric creatures that made the movie a cultural landmark. You can buy it right now if you want your everyday scent to carry some of that iconic chaos.

The Jurassic Park fragrance fits into a growing trend of pop culture brands expanding into unexpected lifestyle categories. Film and TV franchises have spent years moving into food, fashion, and homeware, but fragrance feels like a particularly bold leap. A scent tied to a film about dinosaurs breaking free and eating people is either the most ridiculous idea in beauty history or a stroke of genius, and honestly it might be both.

Jurassic Park as a franchise has stayed remarkably healthy across more than three decades, spawning multiple sequels and the Jurassic World trilogy that brought in billions at the box office. That kind of cultural staying power gives Universal and its licensing partners the confidence to try something as out-there as a perfume. Fans of the original film are now in their thirties, forties, and fifties, and they have money to spend on nostalgia.

If you are curious what cinematic dinosaur chaos actually smells like in bottle form, the Jurassic Park fragrance is available now. It joins a long list of franchise merchandise that proves Hollywood's most beloved properties are no longer just entertainment. They are a full lifestyle.
keyPoints: An official Jurassic Park fragrance is now available to buy
The scent is marketed with the tagline 65 million years in the making
Jurassic Park is Steven Spielberg's 1993 film that launched a massive Hollywood franchise
The launch is part of a wider trend of film franchises moving into lifestyle and beauty products
The Jurassic Park franchise has stayed culturally relevant for over 30 years through sequels and the Jurassic World series
